judgments.comparative_verdict: Both reports independently identify the same root cause, commit, reproduction flaw, and recommendation with essentially equivalent rigor; Report 2 adds a bit more depth (classifier determinism, CI-check suggestion) but both fully satisfy all four expectations.

report_1.evidence: The fix is a one-line change to **`ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml`**: change `expected_label_field: label` to `expected_label_field: gold_label`, so it matches the schema actually used in `fixtures/eval_set.jsonl`

report_2.evidence: The defect is in `ticket-scorer/config/scoring_config.yaml`, line 2: `expected_label_field: label`. ... The fix is a one-line config change: `expected_label_field: gold_label`.

report_1.evidence: The regression began on **2026-06-17**, with commit `53551e8` — "chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)". ... `pipeline/metrics/nightly_scores.csv` shows accuracy holding at 0.90–0.92 through `2026-06-16`, then dropping to exactly `0.00` starting `2026-06-17`

report_2.evidence: The regression began with commit `53551e8` ("chore(eval): migrate eval set to gold_label schema (SCORE-77)", authored 2026-06-17). ... `pipeline/metrics/nightly_scores.csv` corroborates this precisely: accuracy is ~0.90–0.92 every day through 2026-06-16, and is exactly `0.00` every day from 2026-06-17 onward

report_1.evidence: `pipeline/score_one.py` — the script used for the spot-checks — never loads the config or any gold label; it just prints `predict_category(...)` for one ticket. It's structurally incapable of reproducing this bug... To correctly reproduce: run the nightly batch job itself (`pipeline/score_batch.py` via its actual entrypoint/config, not `score_one.py`)

report_2.evidence: The engineer re-ran tickets through `pipeline/score_one.py`, which never reads `config/scoring_config.yaml` and never compares to any gold label at all — it just prints the raw predicted category... Correct reproduction: run the actual nightly entry point end-to-end, e.g. `python -m pipeline.score_batch`

report_1.evidence: I recommend against it. It would not fix anything — the scoring bug is entirely independent of which model produces predictions, since `gold = record.get("label")` returns `None` regardless of what `prediction` is.

report_2.evidence: I recommend against it. There is no "model" involved at all in the sense the note implies — `classifier.py` is pure rule-based keyword matching with `model_id: ticket-classifier-v3` in the config being just a label string, not something that changes prediction behavior via an API call. Rolling back "the model" would not touch the actual defect (the mismatched config key)
